title: Grid Reveal, Container Queries, Variable Font, Elastic Snake, Island Nav
description: Five live UI patterns: clip-path product reveal, @container responsive cards, proximity variable font, elastic cursor spring trail, morphing pill nav.

drops.title: Clip-Path Grid Reveal
drops.description: Hover a product card — a cross-shaped clip-path blooms open to expose a full-bleed detail view while neighbors retreat.
drops.concept: On hover (or tap), a clip-path polygon animates from a collapsed center point to full coverage, revealing a detail layer. CSS :has() dims the sibling cards simultaneously. The whole effect runs with zero JavaScript animation — only CSS transitions and pointer events.
drops.source: Codrops 'Animated Product Grid Preview with GSAP & Clip-Path' (May 2025), reimplemented in vanilla CSS

drops.title: Container Query Cards
drops.description: Drag the slider — cards reflow their own layout from icon-only to full based on their container width, not the viewport.
drops.concept: @container queries let each card element respond to its own container's size rather than the viewport. This demo has four breakpoints (Minimal/Compact/Comfortable/Full) showing how a single card component can serve sidebar, list, and hero contexts with zero JavaScript layout logic.
drops.source: CSS Containment Level 3 / Container Queries — MDN Web Docs & W3C CSSWG

drops.title: Variable Font Proximity
drops.description: Each letter senses cursor distance and responds with font weight — 100 to 900 — via per-letter spring physics.
drops.concept: Each letter span holds a CSS custom property `--w` that drives `font-variation-settings: 'wght' var(--w)`. JavaScript computes per-letter distance to the cursor and spring-lerps toward a target weight. Closer = heavier. The color also shifts from dim lavender to bright purple at max weight. Works with any variable font that exposes a wght axis.
drops.source: Variable font proximity pattern — inspired by demos from the variable fonts community & Chrome DevRel

drops.title: Elastic Cursor Snake
drops.description: A 22-segment cursor trail where each dot spring-follows the previous — elastic, fluid, mesmerizing.
drops.concept: Each segment stores position and velocity. Every frame, it applies a spring force toward the segment ahead (Hooke's law) then damps the velocity. The result is an elastic chain that stretches and oscillates. Head radius tapers to tail, and color cycles through a palette gradient. Tunable in real-time: segment count, stiffness, damping, and palette.
drops.source: Canvas spring-physics cursor trail — a widely-shared creative coding pattern, popularized via Codepen and design portfolios

drops.title: Morphing Island Nav
drops.description: A floating pill navigation where the active indicator flows between tabs with spring easing and the label expands inline.
drops.concept: getBoundingClientRect() measures each tab's position at click time. The pill's `left` and `width` CSS properties animate to match the selected item using a spring cubic-bezier. The active label expands via `max-width: 0 → 80px` transition, pushing other items aside without layout shift. Two variants: expanding-label and dot-ring.
drops.source: Inspired by Apple Dynamic Island and floating tab bar implementations across the open-source design community
